title: Diporidium macrocarpum
taxonomy: Diporidium macrocarpum is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Thymelaeaceae, commonly known as the mezereum family. This family is a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in the Northern Hemisphere. Thymelaeaceae is a relatively small family, consisting of around 40 to 50 genera and approximately 700 species. The family is known for its diverse range of plants, including shrubs, trees, and vines. Diporidium macrocarpum, along with other members of the family, plays a vital role in various ecosystems, providing food and shelter for various species of animals and insects.
